evals.prompt: Check http://localhost:3099/js-errors for any JavaScript issues. Tell me what errors are on this page.
evals.expected_output: Should find: TypeError (uncaught), deprecation warnings. Should use the skill's Playwright script to detect console errors and page errors systematically.

assertions.text: Detects the uncaught TypeError about null properties
Uses structured approach (not just opening URL in browser)

evals.prompt: Run a full QA check on http://localhost:3099/security and tell me about any security issues you find.
evals.expected_output: Should find: secrets in HTML comments, XSS risk, open redirect, permissive CORS, missing CSP, eval() on URL params.

assertions.text: Detects secrets in HTML comments (API_KEY or DB_PASSWORD)
Identifies at least 3 distinct security issues

evals.prompt: Test http://localhost:3099/bad-ecommerce for any design, accessibility, or usability problems. This is an e-commerce page — tell me what's wrong with it.
evals.expected_output: Should find: unprofessional fonts (Comic Sans), invisible button text, low contrast footer, too many font families, small font sizes, clashing colors.

assertions.text: Identifies font problems (Comic Sans or unprofessional)
Provides actionable recommendations (not just listing issues)

evals.expected_output: Should run Phase 1 only (agent-browser smoke test). Should complete quickly and report pass/fail with error count.

assertions.text: Uses agent-browser for quick smoke test
Does NOT run Lighthouse or axe-core (quick mode)

evals.prompt: Generate tests for the codebase in the current directory. I want to know what routes exist and get automated tests for them.
evals.expected_output: Should scan for routes using file-based routing, config-based routing, or link crawling. Should generate Playwright test files and report coverage.
Should run QA at multiple viewports (mobile, tablet, desktop). Should check for horizontal overflow, touch targets, font sizes at each viewport.
Should run lightweight Phase 1+2, output structured JSON with pass/fail, errors array, and duration_ms.
